_comment: ADR-0016 D2: cost is computed from per-thread USAGE (Claude-Platform-sourced,
GET /v1/sessions/{id}/threads) times this git-pinned, model-aware,
cache-bucket-aware price table -- there is NO public per-token price-list API
(confirmed live 2026-07-07: GET /v1/models carries capabilities/max_tokens/effort
but no pricing field at all), so prices must be pinned somewhere. Anti-drift is
handled by (a) isolating every price in this ONE small, reviewed file, each tier
carrying its own effective_from/effective_until, and (b) `harness/cost.py`'s
`--check-pricing-drift` guard, which fails loud when today's date falls outside
every tier's window for a model family.
`cache_multipliers` are fixed and (per ADR-0016 D2's live-confirmed arithmetic,
reproduced exactly by the golden test in tests/test_cost_golden.py) apply
UNIFORMLY across both model families below: a cache-write (creation) token costs
`write_5m`/`write_1h` times the model's OWN base INPUT rate; a cache-read token
costs `read` times that same base input rate. Output happens to price out to
~5x each model's own base input rate on every tier below -- noted as a sanity
observation, NOT stored/derived as a multiplier (output_per_million_usd is
pinned explicitly per tier, not computed, for maximum accuracy per the ADR's
'prices must be pinned, not formula-derived' stance).
Judge methodology v2 (2026-07-07, owner-directed, docs/adr/0016 amendment):
'claude-opus-4-8' was added -- ALL FOUR judges now default to Opus 4.8 (a judge
must be a stronger model than what it judges), verified live against the pricing
page the same day: $5/$25 per MTok in/out, standard (non-introductory) pricing,
no effective_until. Its cache multipliers ($6.25/$10/$0.50) reproduce the SAME
uniform 1.25/2.0/0.1-of-base-input ratios as every other family, so no
per-model cache_multipliers override was needed. Note the pricing page's own
tokenizer caveat: Opus 4.7+ (incl. 4.8) and Sonnet 5 use a newer tokenizer that
produces ~30% MORE tokens for the same text than Haiku 4.5's tokenizer -- part
of why an Opus judge costs more per call than a token-count comparison alone
would suggest, not a pricing.json concern but worth knowing when reading a
judge-cost.json total. The 'web_search' block below is a SEPARATE, flat,
per-call (not per-token, not per-model) rate Anthropic bills web-search tool
invocations at -- confirmed live the same day against the web-search-tool docs
page ('$10 per 1,000 searches' = $0.01/search); web fetch is NOT priced here
because it is billed at token cost only, no separate per-call fee (confirmed
live against the web-fetch-tool docs page: 'available at no additional cost...
you only pay standard token costs').
